L1.area: Server-side session state persistence and storage backend layer

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled session token from an untrusted UI stream payload is used to construct file paths for the disk-based session storage backend without sanitization. By injecting directory traversal sequences into the token, an attacker can read, write, or delete arbitrary files on the server's filesystem outside the intended session storage directory.

L3.area: Disk-based file session storage backend within the server framework
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled session token from an untrusted UI stream payload is used to construct file paths for the disk-based file session storage backend without sanitization. When the framework is configured to use file-based session persistence, injecting directory traversal sequences into the session token allows an attacker to target arbitrary files on the server's filesystem outside the intended session storage directory, enabling denial of service or arbitrary file manipulation.

ground_truth.reason: The FileStateSessionBackend._make_file_path method directly concatenates the user-supplied state_token into a file path without sanitization, allowing path traversal sequences to escape the intended session storage directory.
